definition.en: The true unconscious is not unconscious. Instead, it is so profoundly and unutteringly conscious that it bubbles over.
definition.zh: 真正的无意识并非无意识。相反，它是如此深刻而难以言喻地有意识，以至于满溢而出。

definition.en: My point is that the unconscious is conscious.
definition.zh: 我的要点是：无意识是有意识的。

definition.en: The unconscious is no longer equated with darkness, or with unknown frightening elements. Its character is transformed, so that the 'dark' qualities are seen as actually illuminating portions of conscious life, while also providing great sources of power and energy for normal ego-oriented experience.
definition.zh: 无意识不再等同于黑暗或未知的可怕元素。它的特性被转化了，因此那些“黑暗”特质实际上被视为照亮意识生活的部分，同时为正常的自我导向经验提供巨大的力量与能量来源。

definition.en: The complicated, infinitely varied, unbelievably rich tapestry of Jung's, in quotes 'unconscious,' could hardly be unconscious. It is the product of an inner consciousness with far more sense of identity and purpose than the daily ego.
definition.zh: 荣格所谓的“无意识”那复杂、无限多样、难以置信的丰富织锦，几乎不可能是无意识的。它是一种内在意识的产物，其身份感和目的感远胜于日常自我。

definition.en: The collective unconscious is not static however, and itself interacts and constantly changes. These are all phases of constant creativity within your system. You constantly add to the collective unconscious, and constantly receive from it.
definition.zh: 集体无意识并非静止不变，它自身也在互动并不断变化。这些都是你们系统内持续创造力的各个阶段。你们不断为集体无意识增添内容，也不断从中接收。

explanation: 赛斯认为，无意识并非真正“无意识”，而是拥有比日常自我更强烈身份感和目的性的内在意识。它并非混沌或黑暗，而是高度有序、富有创造力的意识状态，只是日常自我因认知局限而将其视为无意识。无意识是意识的另一面，与清醒自我持续互动，共同构成完整人格。它包含集体无意识，但并非荣格所认为的模糊根源，而是具有清晰组织和深层背景。无意识是创造力的源泉，在梦境中尤为活跃，提供智慧与能量。它处理概率、引导行为，甚至主动选择危机以激发潜能。恐惧和负面联想源于对内在自我的误解；实际上，无意识是清醒生活的照亮者，而非威胁。改变睡眠习惯可增强意识与无意识的融合，使梦境智慧清晰融入现实。无意识并非单一，而是多重心智的体现，

quotes.text: Jung's dark side of the self is the ego, not the unconscious. The complicated, infinitely varied, unbelievably rich tapestry of Jung's, in quotes "unconscious," could hardly be unconscious. It is the product of an inner consciousness with far more sense of identity and purpose than the daily ego. It is the daily ego's ignorance and limited focus that makes it view so-called unconscious activity as chaotic.
quotes.translation: 荣格所说的自我阴暗面是自我，而非无意识。荣格笔下那个复杂多变、难以置信地丰富的所谓"无意识"织锦，几乎不可能是无意识的。它是内在意识的产物，这种意识拥有比日常自我更强烈的身份认同感和目的感。正是日常自我的无知和狭隘焦点，才使它将所谓的无意识活动视为混乱。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Again, when you are in a state that is not the normal waking one, when you have forsaken this daily self, you are nevertheless conscious and alert. You merely block out the memory from the normal waking ego. So when the attributes of consciousness are given, creativity is largely ignored. It is assigned instead primarily to the unconscious. My point is that the unconscious is conscious. Creativity is one of the most important attributes and aspects of consciousness. We will differentiate between normal ego consciousness then, and consciousness that only appears unconscious to that ego.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，当你处于非正常清醒状态时，当你放下这个日常自我时，你依然是有意识且警觉的。你只是屏蔽了正常清醒自我的记忆。因此，当意识的属性被列举时，创造力在很大程度上被忽视了。它反而被主要归因于无意识。我的观点是：无意识是有意识的。创造力是意识最重要属性和面向之一。那么，我们将区分正常的自我意识，以及那个在自我看来只是看似无意识的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Jung's collective unconscious was an attempt to give your world its psychological roots, but Jung1 could not perceive the clarity, organization, and deeper context in which that collective unconscious has its own existence. Reality as Framework 2 is organized in a different fashion than it is in the Framework 1 world, and the processes of reasoning are far quicker. In Framework 1 the reasoning processes work largely by deduction, and they must constantly check their own results against the seemingly concrete experience of physical events. The reasoning of the inner ego is involved with the creative invention of those experiences. It is involved with events in a context of a different kind, for it deals intimately with probabilities.
quotes.translation: 荣格的集体无意识试图为你们的世界赋予心理根源，但荣格无法看清那个集体无意识自身存在所依托的清晰性、组织性和更深层背景。作为框架二中的现实，其组织方式与框架一世界不同，推理过程也快得多。在框架一中，推理过程主要依靠演绎，且必须不断根据看似具体的物理事件经验来检验自身结果。内在自我的推理则涉及对这些经验的创造性发明。它在一个不同性质的语境中参与事件，因为它与可能性密切互动。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are many other natural and spontaneous kinds of comprehension that can also result from the waking and sleeping rhythms that I have suggested. The unconscious, the color black, and death all have strongly negative connotations in which the inner self is feared; the dream state is mistrusted and often suggests thoughts of both death and/or evil. But changed wake-sleep habits can, again, bring about a transformation in which it is obvious that dreams contain great wisdom and creativity, that the unconscious is indeed quite conscious, and that in fact the individual sense of identity can be retained in the dream state. The fear of self-annihilation, symbolically thought of as death, can then no longer apply as it did before.
quotes.translation: 还有许多其他自然且自发的理解方式，也可以从我建议的清醒与睡眠节奏中产生。无意识、黑色和死亡都具有强烈的负面含义，其中内在自我被恐惧；梦境状态不被信任，且常常引发对死亡和/或邪恶的联想。但改变清醒-睡眠习惯，可以再次带来转变，使人们明显看到梦境蕴含着巨大的智慧和创造力，无意识确实相当有意识，而且事实上，个体身份认同感可以在梦境状态中得以保留。对自我湮灭的恐惧（象征性地被视为死亡）便不再像以前那样适用了。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Because of my own writing experience, I'm also well aware of the process involved in translating unconscious material into conscious reality. It's particularly obvious when I'm working on poetry. Whatever else is involved in Seth's book, certainly some kind of unconscious activity is operating at high gear. It was only natural, then, that I found myself comparing my own conscious creative experience with the trance procedure involved in Seth's book. I wanted to discover why I felt that Seth's book was his, as divorced from mine. If both were coming from the same unconscious, then why the subjective differences in my feelings?
quotes.translation: 基于我自己的写作经验，我也很清楚将无意识材料转化为意识现实的过程。在我创作诗歌时这一点尤为明显。无论赛斯书中还涉及什么，某种无意识活动肯定在高速运转。因此，我自然而然地发现自己将个人的意识创作经验与赛斯书中涉及的出神过程进行比较。我想找出为什么我觉得赛斯的书是他的，而非我的。如果两者都来自同一个无意识，那么为什么我的感受会有主观差异？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: My remark has nothing to do with your accepted concepts of the unconscious portions of the self. Your ideas of the unconscious are so linked to your limited ideas of personhood as to be meaningless in this discussion. It is as if you used only one finger of one hand, and then said: "This is the proper expression of my personhood." It is not just that there are other functions of the mind, unused, but that in those terms you have other minds. You have one brain, it is true, but you allow it to use only one station, or to identify itself with only one mind of many.
quotes.translation: 我的说法与你们对自我无意识部分的既有概念毫无关联。你们对无意识的认知与你们对人格的狭隘理解紧密相连，以至于在这场讨论中毫无意义。这就像你们只用一只手的一根手指，然后宣称："这是我人格的恰当表达。"问题不仅在于心智的其他功能未被使用，更在于按你们的说法，你们拥有多重心智。你们确实只有一个大脑，却只允许它使用一个频道，或只让它认同众多心智中的某一个。
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To some extent, there is a natural and spontaneous merging of what you would think of as conscious and unconscious activity. This in itself brings about a greater understanding of the give-and-take that exists between the ego and other portions of the self. The unconscious is no longer equated with darkness, or with unknown frightening elements. Its character is transformed, so that the "dark" qualities are seen as actually illuminating portions of conscious life, while also providing great sources of power and energy for normal ego-oriented experience.
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，你们所谓的意识与无意识活动之间存在自然自发的融合。这本身就能促进对自我与其他自我部分之间互动关系的更深理解。无意识不再等同于黑暗或未知的可怕元素。它的特质被转化，使得那些"黑暗"特质实际上成为意识生活的照明部分，同时为正常的自我导向体验提供巨大的力量与能量源泉。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Often, for instance, you handle probabilities very well, while remaining consciously blind to them because of your concepts. Even then, however, on other levels your unconscious reaction will follow your own conscious intents. You may make a move in physical life, for example, seemingly for one reason. You may also be unconsciously reacting to quite pertinent data regarding the probable actions of others. Because you do not really fully accept the fact that you can so react, you may block this unofficial information on the one hand, even while on the other you take it into consideration. You are far more aware than you realize of the probable future in areas with which you are concerned. This is true on all levels. If your purposes do not involve illness, for instance, and yet if you believe in contagion, you will automatically avoid circumstances that can lead to epidemics. In terms of probabilities that particular kind will not enter your experience.
quotes.translation: 例如，你们常常能很好地处理或然性，却因自身观念而在意识层面对其视而不见。即便如此，在其他层面上，你们的无意识反应仍会遵循自身的意识意图。比如在物质生活中，你们可能看似出于某个原因采取行动，同时也在无意识中对他人可能行为的相关数据做出反应。由于你们并未真正完全接受自己能如此反应的事实，一方面可能会屏蔽这些非官方信息，另一方面却又在考虑它们。你们对自己所关注领域的或然未来的觉察，远比自认为的更为敏锐。这在所有层面都成立。例如，若你们的目的不涉及疾病，却又相信传染，便会自动避开可能导致疫情的环境。从或然性角度来看，那种特定情况根本不会进入你们的经验。
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You felt before, unconsciously, that you were drifting and that life had little meaning. Beneath the surface of events you felt unfulfilled, and felt that you had great courage and abilities with never a chance to use them, and no "heroic" episodes then to rouse you to fuller understanding, and no real impetus to lift you or to bring excitement into your days. Unconsciously, therefore, you chose a situation in which a crisis was precipitated, rousing all the greatest elements of heart and soul, so that they must strain to understand, to perceive, to triumph. And so you shall, in whatever way is most important for you, and you will learn more and be more fulfilled than you would have been had those conditions not been initiated.
quotes.translation: 你们此前在无意识中感到自己在随波逐流，生活毫无意义。在事件表象之下，你们感到未获满足，觉得自己拥有巨大勇气和能力却永无施展机会，没有"英雄式"的插曲来唤醒你们更深的领悟，也没有真正的动力来提升你们或为日子注入激情。因此，你们在无意识中选择了一个会引发危机的处境，激发心灵与灵魂中最伟大的元素，迫使它们奋力去理解、去洞察、去胜利。你们必将如此，以对你们最重要的方式，并且会比未启动这些条件时学到更多、获得更深的满足。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The division between the two aspects of experience begins to take on the characteristics of completely diverse behavior. The unconscious becomes more and more unfamiliar to consciousness. Those beliefs build up about it, and the symbolisms involved are exaggerated. The unknown seems to be threatening and degenerate. The color black assumes stronger tendencies in its connection with evil -- something to be avoided. Self-annihilation seems to be a threat ever-present in the dream or sleep state. At the same time all of those flamboyant, creative, spontaneous, emotional surges that emerge normally from the unconscious become feared and projected outward, then, upon enemies, other races and creeds.
quotes.translation: 经验这两个层面之间的分野开始呈现出完全不同的行为特征。无意识对意识而言变得越来越陌生。关于它的信念逐渐累积，相关的象征意义被夸大。未知似乎充满威胁且堕落。黑色与邪恶的关联愈发强烈——成为需要回避之物。自我毁灭似乎成为梦境或睡眠状态中始终存在的威胁。与此同时，所有那些从无意识中自然涌现的华丽、创造、自发、情感涌动，都变得令人恐惧，并被投射到外部——投射到敌人、其他种族和信仰之上。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your conscious mind can only accept a certain sequence of probabilities as recognized experience. As I have said, the choices among probabilities go on constantly, both on conscious and unconscious levels. Events that you do not perceive as conscious experience are (pause) a part of your unconscious experience, however, to some extent. This applies to the individual, and of course en masse the same applies to world events. Each action seeks all of its own possible fulfillments. All That Is seeks all possible experience, but in such a larger framework in this case that questions of, say, pain or death simply do not apply, though [certainly] they do on the physical level (all quite forcefully).
quotes.translation: 你们的意识心智只能接受特定序列的或然性作为被认可的经验。如我所说，对或然性的选择持续进行，既在意识层面也在无意识层面。你们未将其感知为意识经验的事件，在某种程度上却是你们无意识经验的一部分。这适用于个体，当然也适用于世界事件。每个行动都寻求自身所有可能的实现。一切万有寻求所有可能的经验，但在如此宏大的框架中，诸如痛苦或死亡之类的问题根本不适用——尽管在物质层面它们确实存在（语气相当有力）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The collective unconscious is not static however, and itself interacts and constantly changes. These are all phases of constant creativity within your system. You constantly add to the collective unconscious, and constantly receive from it. You change what you receive, however. Each physical object has a psychic effect on all other objects, and a psychic existence that is independent of its physical existence.
quotes.translation: 然而集体潜意识并非静止不动，它本身也在互动并持续变化。这些全是你们系统内不断创造的不同阶段。你们不断为集体潜意识增添内容，也不断从中接收信息。但你们会改变所接收的内容。每个物质客体对其他所有客体都有心灵影响，并且拥有独立于其物质存在的心灵实存。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Quite the contrary, the ego is replenished, and rather directly. (Pause.) It is the fear that the unconscious, so-called, is chaotic, that causes psychologists to make such statements, and there (Pause) is also something in the nature of those who practice psychology, a fascination, in many cases, already predisposed to fear the so-called unconscious in direct proportion to its attraction for them.
quotes.translation: 恰恰相反，自我反而得到补给，而且是相当直接的补给。（停顿）正是对所谓潜意识的恐惧——认为它是混乱的——导致心理学家做出这类论断，而且（停顿）从事心理学研究的人本身也有某种特质，在许多案例中，他们早已倾向于对所谓潜意识既恐惧又着迷，且恐惧程度与吸引力成正比。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The true unconscious is not unconscious. Instead, it is so profoundly and unutteringly conscious that it bubbles over. The life that you know is simply one of the many areas in which it is conscious. In each facet of its consciousness, literally tremendous power and balance must be maintained to hold aloft this particular consciousness-experience from all others.
quotes.translation: 真正的潜意识并非无意识。相反，它如此深邃、难以言喻地有意识，以至于满溢而出。你们所认知的生命，不过是它意识所及的众多领域之一。在其意识的每个面向中，都必须维持惊人的力量与平衡，才能将这种特定的意识体验从其他所有体验中托举出来。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Did those "genetically inferior," for example, have the right to reproduce?1 Illness was thought to come like a storm, the result of physical forces against which the individual had little recourse. The "new" Freudian ideas of the unsavory unconscious led further to a new dilemma, for it was then--as it is now--widely believed that as the result of experiences in infancy the subconscious, or unconscious, might very well sabotage the best interests of the conscious personality, and trick it into illness and disaster.
quotes.translation: 例如，那些"基因低劣者"是否有权繁衍后代？疾病曾被视为如风暴般降临，是个人几乎无法抗拒的物理力量所致。弗洛伊德关于"不洁潜意识"的新观念进一步引发了新的困境，因为当时——正如现在——人们普遍相信，由于婴儿期的经历，潜意识或下意识很可能破坏意识人格的最佳利益，诱使其陷入疾病与灾难。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He does all the things and says all the things that Augustus One would dearly love to do and say, with only certain safeguards. Augustus One, however, is not literally unconscious during this time, but quite aware of the "vicarious" activities and fulfillments. Again, it is a game of hide-and-seek, in which the so-called unconscious mind is relatively innocent.
quotes.translation: 他做着奥古斯都一号渴望做的一切事情，说着他渴望说的一切话语，只是带有某些安全措施。然而奥古斯都一号在此期间并非真正无意识，而是相当清楚这些"替代性"活动与满足。这又是一场捉迷藏游戏，其中所谓的潜意识相对无辜。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Emotion and imagination are being considered as far superior. The displaced powers of consciousness are still being assigned to the unconscious, and great efforts are being made to reach what seem to be normally inaccessible areas of awareness. To this end drugs are utilized, cults set up, and there are methods and training manuals galore. Period. Yet there is nothing basically inaccessible about such "inner knowledge or experience." It can all be quite conscious, and utilized to enrich the reality that you know. The conscious mind is not some prodigal child or poor relative of the self. It can quite freely focus into inner reality when you understand that it can. You, again, have a conscious mind. You can change the focus of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 情感与想象力正被视作远为优越之物。被置换的意识力量仍被归诸潜意识，人们正竭尽全力触及那些看似通常无法触及的意识领域。为此使用药物、建立教派，还有数不清的方法与训练手册。句号。然而这类"内在知识或体验"本质上并非不可触及。它们完全可以被清醒认知，并用于丰富你们所知的实相。意识心智并非自我的败家子或穷亲戚。当你们明白它能够聚焦内在实相时，它完全可以自由地做到这一点。再说一次，你们拥有意识心智。你们可以改变自身意识的焦点。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You are aware, alert, and participating in many more realities than you know as your soul expresses itself through you. That consciousness of your usual daylight hours, the ego consciousness, rises up like a flower from the ground of the "underneath," the unconscious bed of your own reality. Though you are not aware of it, this ego itself emerges, then falls back again into the unconscious, from which another ego then rises as a new bloom from the springtime earth.
quotes.translation: 你觉察、警觉并参与的现实远比你已知的更多，因为你的灵魂正透过你表达自己。你日常白昼的意识——即自我意识——如同花朵般从“底层”的土壤中升起，这土壤便是你自身实相的无意识之床。尽管你并未察觉，这个自我本身浮现后又落回无意识中，接着另一个自我如春日大地的新芽般再度升起。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: If such information is given to you by another, by a psychic, for example, that individual is also very apt to pick up those "lives" that make sense to you now, and -- unconsciously of course -- to structure them precisely along the lines of your beliefs. This may not seem obvious. (Emphatically:) If an individual believes that he is basically unworthy he will recall, or be given, those lives that justify that idea. If he thinks he must pay for his sins now, then that belief will attract memory of those lives that will reinforce it; this will be highly organized recall, leaving out everything that does not apply.
quotes.translation: 若这类信息由他人——例如一位通灵者——提供给你，此人也很可能捕捉到那些对你当下有意义的“前世”，并且——当然是无意识地——精确地按照你的信念结构来组织它们。这或许并不明显。（强调地：）若一个人相信自己本质上毫无价值，他将忆起或被告知那些合理化此念头的生命；若他认为必须为今生的罪孽付出代价，那么此信念将吸引强化它的生命记忆——这将是一种高度组织化的回忆，排除了所有不相关的内容。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When you are determined to manipulate your environment, then you separate yourself from it. Since you are part of it, this also leads you to try to place yourself apart from your own subjective reality. It is quite possible to take your normally conscious "I" into the dream state, to your advantage. When you do this you will see that the dreaming "I" and the waking "I" are one, but operating in entirely different environments. Therefore, you become familiar with depths of experience and knowledge unknown to you before. You acquire a true flexibility and expanded awareness of your own being, and open channels of communication between your waking and dreaming realities. This means that you are far better able to utilize unconscious knowledge, and also to acquaint the unconscious with your present physical situation.
quotes.translation: 当你决心操控环境时，你便将自己与环境分离。既然你是环境的一部分，这也会导致你试图将自身与主观实相割裂。将你通常清醒的“我”带入梦境状态是完全可能的，且对你有利。当你这样做时，你会看见做梦的“我”与清醒的“我”本为一体，只是运作于完全不同的环境中。因此，你将熟悉此前未知的经验与知识深度，获得对自身存在的真正灵活性与扩展觉知，并开启清醒与梦境实相之间的沟通渠道。这意味着你能更好地运用无意识知识，同时让无意识了解你当前的物质情境。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Throughout the ages some have recognized the fact that there is self consciousness and purpose in certain dream and sleep states, and have maintained, even in waking life, the sense of continuity of this inner self. To such people it is no longer possible to identify completely with the ego consciousness. They are too obviously aware of themselves as more. When such knowledge is gained, the ego can accept it, for it finds to its surprise that it is not less conscious, but more conscious, that its limitations are dissipated; now it is not true, and I emphasize this strongly, that so-called unconscious material, given any freedom, will draw energy away from the egotistically organized self in a normal personality.
quotes.translation: 历代以来，有些人已认识到某些梦境与睡眠状态中存在自我意识与目的，甚至在清醒生活中也保持着这种内在自我的连续性。对这些人而言，再也不可能完全认同于自我意识。他们太清楚地意识到自己远不止于此。当获得此类知识时，自我能够接纳它，因为它惊讶地发现自己并非意识减弱，而是意识增强，其局限被消解；我强烈强调：所谓无意识材料若被赋予任何自由，绝不会从正常人格中由自我组织的那部分抽取能量——这并非事实。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Only when such nourishment is for some reason cut off to a considerable degree, is the ego threatened by starvation... We will have more to say concerning the ego's relationship with the so-called unconscious. In a healthy personality the inner self easily projects all experience into EE units, where they are translated into actuality. Physical matter therefore acts as a feedback. Now we will end our session unless you have questions.
quotes.translation: 只有当这种滋养因某种原因被严重切断时，自我才会面临饥饿的威胁……关于自我与所谓无意识的关系，我们还将进一步探讨。在健康的人格中，内在自我轻松地将所有经验投射为EE单位，在那里它们被转化为实相。因此物质身体充当了反馈机制。若无问题，我们现在结束此课。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The fly is intensely conscious, at every moment engrossed in itself and its environment, precisely tuned to elements of which you are "unconscious." There are simply different kinds of consciousness, and you cannot basically compare one to the other any more than you can compare, say, a toad to a star to an apple to a thought to a woman to a child to a native to a suburbanite to a spider to a cat. They are varieties of consciousness, each focused upon its own view of reality, each containing experience that others exclude.
quotes.translation: 苍蝇具有强烈的意识，每时每刻都沉浸于自身与环境之中，精准调谐于你“无意识”的元素。只是存在不同种类的意识，你无法从根本上比较它们，正如你无法比较蟾蜍与星辰、苹果与思想、女人与孩童、土著与郊区居民、蜘蛛与猫。它们是意识的变体，各自聚焦于自身的实相视角，各自包含他人所排除的经验。
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (11:02.) Give us a moment ... I told you (in the last session) that in one probability Ruburt was a nun, expressing mysticism in a highly disciplined context, where it must be watched so that it does not get out of hand. Because there is an unconscious flow of information and experience here, you have one of the reasons for Ruburt's caution in some psychic matters, and his fear of leading people astray. There were three offshoots: one, the nun, with mysticism conventionally expressed, but under guarded circumstances; one, the writer who veiled mystical experience through art; and one, the Ruburt you know, who experienced mystical experience directly, teaches others to do the same, and forms through writing a wedding of the two aspects. You have known two of those selves, then, and you were present at Ruburt's birth with Idea Construction.
quotes.translation: （11:02。）请稍等片刻……我告诉过你（在上节课中），在一种可能性中，鲁柏曾是一名修女，在高度纪律化的环境中表达神秘主义，这种环境必须被严密监控，以防失控。由于这里存在着无意识的信息与经验流动，这便是鲁柏在某些灵性事务上保持谨慎、并害怕误导他人的原因之一。有三个分支：其一，修女，以传统方式表达神秘主义，但在受保护的环境下；其二，那位通过艺术掩盖神秘体验的作家；其三，你所认识的鲁柏，他直接体验神秘经验，教导他人也这样做，并通过写作将这两方面结合。那么，你已认识其中两个自我，并且在“意念建构”中见证了鲁柏的诞生。
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: According to the intensity of the situation, now, another also desirable solution may be worked out in a probable reality. On an unconscious level you are aware of your probable selves, and they of you. You share the same psychic roots, and your joint yet separate dreams are available to "all of you." This does not mean that you are dreaming someone else's dream, any more than it means that twins, for example, do. It does mean that your probable selves and you share in a body of symbolism, background, and ability. The multistructured nature of the dream state allows for dream dramas in which probable selves do appear. They may appear as symbolically representing strong characteristics upon which they have focused, though you have ignored them.
quotes.translation: 根据情境的强度，现在，另一种同样可取的解决方案可能在一种可能性实相中被制定出来。在无意识层面上，你意识到你的可能性自我，它们也意识到你。你们共享相同的灵性根源，而你们共同却又分离的梦境对“你们所有人”都是可及的。这并不意味着你在做别人的梦，正如这并不意味着双胞胎在做彼此的梦一样。它确实意味着你的可能性自我与你共享一套象征、背景和能力的体系。梦境的多元结构本质允许出现可能性自我参与其中的梦境戏剧。它们可能以象征形式出现，代表它们所专注的强烈特质，尽管你忽略了这些特质。
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Intently:) I am saying that the individual self must become consciously aware of far more reality; that it must allow its recognition of identity to expand so that it includes previously unconscious knowledge. To do this you must understand, again, that man must move beyond the concepts of one god, one self, one body, one world, as these ideas are currently understood.5 You are now poised, in your terms, upon a threshold from which the race can go many ways. There are species of consciousness. Your species is in a time of change. There are potentials within the body's mechanisms, in your terms, not as yet used. Developed, they can immeasurably enrich the race, and bring it to levels of spiritual and psychic and physical fulfillment. If some changes are not made, the race as such will not endure.
quotes.translation: （专注地：）我在说，个体自我必须有意识地认识到远更多的实相；它必须允许其对身份的认知扩展，以包含先前无意识的知识。要做到这一点，你必须再次理解，人类必须超越一神、一我、一身、一世界的概念，正如这些观念目前被理解的那样。5 以你们的说法，你们正站在一个门槛上，人类可以从这里走向许多方向。存在着意识的物种。你们的物种正处于变革时期。以你们的说法，身体机制内存在着尚未被使用的潜能。如果这些潜能得到发展，它们将无可估量地丰富人类，并将其带到灵性、心灵和身体圆满的层次。如果不做出某些改变，人类这个物种将无法存续。
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Think of your ideas about your own sexuality in connection with those about your being and consciousness. Regroup your ideas so that you automatically think of sexuality in relationship to your religions and sciences. You have associated the word "female" with the unconscious, while you have been working toward what you now think of as an egotistically based consciousness. There is more in what I am saying than you presently realize. You cannot, each of you, consider the real meaning of your own sexuality unless you understand your own religious history. Follow through, in your own minds, with your memories. Try to be honest with yourselves as to those early experiences in which you forced yourselves to behave differently than you were, because adults told you that you must ... You had better understand the beautiful, unique quality of your own individuality lest you project upon the other sex -- whichever sex you are -- those abilities and qualities that you are afraid are your own, or project upon them those abilities and qualities that you wish you possessed and fear you do not.
quotes.translation: 将你对自己性别的看法与你对自己存在和意识的看法联系起来思考。重新组织你的观念，使你自动将性别与你的宗教和科学联系起来。你们将“女性”一词与无意识联系在一起，而你们一直在努力朝向你们现在所认为的以自我为基础的意识。我所说的内容比你们目前意识到的更多。你们每个人，除非了解自己的宗教历史，否则无法思考自己性别的真正含义。在你们自己的心智中，跟随你们的记忆追溯。尽量对自己诚实，回忆那些早期经历，在其中你们强迫自己表现得与真实自我不同，因为成年人告诉你们必须这样做……你们最好理解自己个体性的美丽而独特的品质，以免你们将那些你们害怕属于自己的能力和品质投射到异性身上——无论你们是哪种性别——或者将那些你们希望拥有却害怕自己没有的能力和品质投射到他们身上。
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In certain terms, then, this involves in a very small way the creation and colonization of a different kind of reality -- consciously accepted, however, from your perspective. On an unconscious level, the world as you know it expands in just such a fashion.10 Several students have had dreams involving their participation in such a project. Ruburt found himself in an out-of-body state, looking at a jacket. It had four rectangular pockets. It was giant-sized. As he looked at it the front flap was open. In the dream he flew through this flap literally into another dimension, where the point of the flap was a hill upon which he landed. From that second perspective, the pockets of the jacket in the first perspective became the windows of a building that existed in a still-further, third dimension beyond the hill. Standing on the hill, he knew that in Perspective One the windows of the building in Perspective Three were jacket pockets, but he could no longer perceive them as such. Looking out from the hill in Perspective Two, Perspective One was invisibly behind him, and Perspective Three was still "ahead" of him, separated from him by a gulf he did not understand.
quotes.translation: 那么，从某种角度来说，这以一种非常小的方式涉及一种不同实相的创造和殖民——然而，从你们的视角来看，这是被有意识地接受的。在无意识层面上，你们所知的世界正是以这种方式扩展。10 几位学生曾做过涉及参与这样一个项目的梦。鲁柏发现自己处于出体状态，看着一件夹克。它有四个长方形口袋。它尺寸巨大。当他看着它时，前襟是敞开的。在梦中，他直接飞过这个襟翼，进入另一个维度，在那里襟翼的尖端成为他降落的一座山丘。从这第二个视角来看，第一个视角中夹克的口袋变成了存在于山丘之外更远、第三个维度中的一栋建筑的窗户。站在山丘上，他知道在视角一中，视角三中建筑的窗户是夹克口袋，但他再也无法如此感知它们。从视角二的山丘向外看，视角一在他身后不可见，而视角三仍在他“前方”，与他相隔一道他不理解的鸿沟。
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner ego is fully conscious. It is a portion of you, however, that deals with the formation of events, that glories in a rather rambunctious and creative activity that your specifications of time and place physically preclude. The unconscious, so-called, is -- and I have said this before2 -- quite conscious, but in another realm of activity. There must be a psychological chamber between these two portions of the self, however -- these seemingly undifferentiated areas, in which back-and-forth translations can occur. Dream periods provide that service, of course, so that in dreams the two egos can meet and merge to some extent, comparing notes like strangers who perhaps meet on a train at night, and are amazed to discover, after some conversation, that they are indeed close relatives, each embarked upon the same journey though seemingly they travelled alone.
quotes.translation: 内在自我是完全有意识的。然而，它是你的一部分，处理事件的形成，并在一种相当喧闹而富有创造性的活动中欢欣鼓舞，这种活动在物理上被你们对时间和地点的具体规定所排除。所谓的无意识是——我以前说过这一点2——相当有意识的，但在另一个活动领域中。然而，在这两个自我部分之间——这些看似未分化的区域——必须有一个心理隔间，在其中可以进行来回的翻译。梦境时期当然提供了这种服务，因此在梦中，两个自我可以在某种程度上相遇并融合，像陌生人一样交换笔记，也许在夜晚的火车上相遇，经过一番交谈后惊讶地发现，他们实际上是近亲，各自踏上了相同的旅程，尽管看似独自旅行。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In more mundane terms, impulses often come from unconscious knowledge, then. This knowledge is spontaneously and automatically received by the energy that composes your body, and then it is processed so that pertinent information applying to you can be taken advantage of. Ideally (underlined), your impulses are always in response to your own best interests -- and, again, to the best interests of your world as well. Obviously there is a deep damaging distrust of impulses in the contemporary world, as in your terms there has been throughout the history that you follow. (Pause.) Impulses are spontaneous, and you have been taught not to trust the spontaneous portions of your being, but to rely upon your reason and your intellect -- which (amused) both operate, incidentally, quite spontaneously, by the way.
quotes.translation: 用更通俗的话来说，冲动往往来自无意识的知识。这种知识会自发且自动地被构成你身体的能量接收，然后经过处理，使适用于你的相关信息得以利用。理想情况下（强调），你的冲动总是回应你自身的最佳利益——而且再次强调，也回应你世界的最佳利益。显然，在当代世界中，人们对冲动存在一种深具破坏性的不信任，就像在你们所遵循的历史中一直存在的那样。（停顿。）冲动是自发的，而你们被教导不要信任自身存在的自发部分，而要依赖你们的理性和智力——顺便说（带着笑意），这两者其实也是相当自发地运作的。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In highly complicated cellular structures like yourselves (pause), with your unique mental properties, you end up with a vital inborn sense of shape and form. The ability to draw is a natural outgrowth of this sensing of shape, this curiosity of form. On a quite unconscious level you possess a biological self-image that is quite different from the self that you see in a mirror. It is a knowledge of bodily form from the inside out, so to speak, composed of cellular shapes and organizations, operating at the maximum. The simple cell, again, has a curiosity about its environment, and on your much more advanced cellular level your own curiosity is unbounded. It is primarily felt as a curiosity about shapes: the urge to touch, to explore, to feel edges and smooth places.
quotes.translation: 在像你们这样高度复杂的细胞结构（停顿）中，凭借你们独特的心理属性，你们最终拥有一种与生俱来的对形状和形态的敏锐感知。绘画能力正是这种形状感知和形式好奇心的自然产物。在相当无意识的层面上，你们拥有一个生物性的自我形象，这与你们在镜中看到的自我截然不同。可以说，这是一种从内而外对身体形态的认知，由细胞形状和组织构成，并以最大效能运作。简单的细胞再次对其环境充满好奇，而在你们更高级的细胞层面上，你们的好奇心是无止境的。它主要体现为对形状的好奇：触摸、探索、感受边缘和光滑表面的冲动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The tree is also inner aware of its environment to an astonishing degree. It maintains contact awareness and the ability to manipulate itself in two completely different worlds, so to speak, one in which it meets little resistance growing upward, and one composed of much heavier elements into which it must grow downward. Man needs artificial methods for example to operate effectively on land or in water, but the so-called unconscious tree manages very nicely in two worlds as diverse certainly as land and water, and makes himself a part of each. I am speaking now of a tree as a "he" for reasons that I will go into in a further discussion.
quotes.translation: 树木也对其环境有着惊人的内在感知。可以说，它保持着接触意识，并能在两个完全不同的世界中操控自身：一个世界是它向上生长时遇到的阻力很小，另一个世界则由更重的元素组成，它必须向下生长进入其中。人类需要人工方法才能有效地在陆地或水中运作，但所谓无意识的树木却能很好地管理两个世界，这两个世界无疑像陆地和海洋一样截然不同，并且它使自己成为每个世界的一部分。我现在把树木称为"他"，原因将在后续讨论中详述。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Even with so-called mental disorders, however, orientation with the body is very important, as are the individual's beliefs about his own form and its relationship with others and with time and space. (Pause.) There will often be chemical imbalances in such a situation, unconsciously produced by the individual, sometimes in order to allow him to work out a series of hallucinatory events. Such sustained "objectified dreaming" necessitates a change, chemically, from the normal state of waking consciousness. It is important to note that regardless of the mental or physical illness adopted, it is chosen for a reason, and is a natural method that the individual himself knows he is physically and mentally equipped to handle.
quotes.translation: 然而，即使是在所谓的精神障碍中，与身体的协调也非常重要，就像个人对自己形态的信念及其与他人、时间和空间的关系一样重要。（停顿。）在这种情况下，通常会出现化学失衡，由个体无意识地产生，有时是为了让他能够经历一系列幻觉事件。这种持续的"客体化梦境"需要从正常清醒意识状态发生化学变化。值得注意的是，无论采取何种精神或身体疾病，它都是出于某种原因被选择的，并且是个体知道自己身心有能力处理的一种自然方法。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ego consciousness must now be familiarized with its roots, or it will turn into something else. You are in a position where your private experience of yourself does not correlate with what you are told by your societies, churches, sciences, archaeologies, or other disciplines. Man's "unconscious" knowledge is becoming more and more consciously apparent. This will be done under and with the direction of an enlightened and expanding egotistical awareness (much louder), that can organize the hereto neglected knowledge -- or it will be done at the expense of the reasoning intellect (again louder), leading to a rebirth of superstition, chaos, and the unnecessary war between reason and intuitive knowledge.
quotes.translation: 自我意识现在必须熟悉其根源，否则它将变成别的东西。你们正处于这样一个境地：你们对自身的私人体验与你们的社会、教会、科学、考古学或其他学科告诉你们的不相符。人类的"无意识"知识正变得越来越有意识地明显。这将在开明且扩展的自我意识（声音更大）的指导和带领下完成，这种意识能够组织迄今被忽视的知识——否则它将牺牲理性智力（再次提高声音）为代价，导致迷信、混乱以及理性与直觉知识之间不必要的战争的重生。
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In other words, portions of the inner self have joined the strictly egotistical functions. The ego in such cases is so attuned that it becomes almost something else. We are coming here toward a definition of illumination in psychic terms. The ego is not, underlined, banished. It joins with portions of the inner self previously unconscious, and illuminates the whole personality. It is no longer primarily physically oriented. Therefore it is no longer an ego in the terms usually meant.
quotes.translation: 换句话说，内在自我的部分已经加入了严格的自我功能。在这种情况下，自我如此协调，以至于它几乎变成了别的东西。我们在这里正接近对灵性术语中"启迪"的定义。自我并未被驱逐（强调）。它与先前无意识的内在自我部分结合，并照亮了整个个性。它不再主要面向物质。因此，它不再是通常意义上的自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: In terms of probabilities, therefore, you choose certain acts, unconsciously transform these into physical events or objects, and then perceive them. But those unchosen events also go out from you and are projected into these other forms. Now the behavior of atoms and molecules is involved here, for again these are only present within your universe during certain stages. Their activity is perceived only during the range of particular vibratory rhythms. When your scientists examine them for example, they do not examine the nature, say, of an atom. They only explore the characteristics of an atom as it acts or shows itself within your system. Its greater reality completely escapes them.
quotes.translation: 就可能性而言，你选择某些行动，无意识地将它们转化为物理事件或物体，然后感知它们。但那些未被选择的事件同样从你身上发出，投射到这些其他形式中。原子和分子的行为也涉及其中，因为它们只在你宇宙的特定阶段存在。它们的活动仅在特定振动节奏的范围内被感知。例如，当你的科学家研究原子时，他们并非在研究原子的本质，而只是探索原子在你系统内运作或显现时的特性。其更宏大的现实完全逃过了他们的注意。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am a personified energy source -- but so are you. I have had many lives, in your terms, yet in other terms I have not lived physically, but rather lent or loaned my energy to lives that rose from my reality but were not me. In the same way you give birth to dream images of your own -- hardly aware that you have done so, unconscious of the fact that you have provided impetus for a kind of psychological reality that quite escapes your notice. The dream stories you begin continue on their own. No dream is stillborn. Each chapter of this book is written in such a way that the ideas presented will activate your own intuitions, and open pathways between your dreaming and waking states.
quotes.translation: 我是一个人格化的能量源头——但你也是。用你们的话说，我经历过许多生世，但从其他角度而言，我并未以肉身生活，而是将我的能量借给那些源于我现实却并非我自身的生命。同样地，你也会生出自己的梦境影像——几乎意识不到自己已这样做，无觉于自己为一种完全逃过你注意的心理现实提供了动力。你开启的梦境故事会自行延续。没有梦是死产的。本书的每一章都以这样的方式写成，使呈现的观念能激活你的直觉，并打开你梦境与清醒状态之间的通道。
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Again Ruburt "tuned in," altered his state of consciousness, and was told not to take them. Joseph was not to have them either. The unconscious knowledge was given (pause), and statements of each body's condition. Both were safe as long as shots were not taken. In this case Ruburt and Joseph acted in direct contradiction to authoritative radio statements, and held their own despite the fact that others in the immediate environment rushed off to the medical centers. They placed their lives on the line. Only an hour later the radio announcements completely changed; people were told that they did not need shots, and that indeed the inoculations could cause severe reactions.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏再次"调频"，改变了他的意识状态，被告知不要接种疫苗。约瑟也不应接种。无意识的知识被给予（停顿），并陈述了每具身体的状况。只要不接种疫苗，两人都是安全的。在此案例中，鲁柏和约瑟直接违背了权威的广播声明，坚持己见，尽管周围其他人纷纷涌向医疗中心。他们将自己的生命置于险境。仅一小时后，广播声明完全改变；人们被告知不需要接种疫苗，而且接种确实可能引起严重反应。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It has been truthfully said that the "unconscious" is intimately aware of the most minute details of your health, state of mind, and relationships. "It" is also aware of the state of the earth's health -- even of the environmental conditions on the other side of the planet. It is familiar with the cultural climate also. Your recognized consciousness operates as it does because of immense information-gathering procedures -- procedures that unite all species. Biologically such information is coded, but that physical information, such as in the genes and chromosomes, can be altered through experience and mental activity in other species as well as your own.
quotes.translation: 诚然，"无意识"密切知晓你健康、心态和关系的最细微细节。"它"也知晓地球的健康状况——甚至地球另一边的环境条件。它还熟悉文化氛围。你意识到的意识之所以如此运作，是因为庞大的信息收集程序——这些程序联合了所有物种。生物上，这类信息被编码，但那些物理信息，如基因和染色体中的信息，可以通过经验和你自身及其他物种的心智活动而改变。
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They can impress others with greater import through their communications. They can move from inner to outer reality with easy ability. They know instinctively how to use symbolism. They are highly creative on an unconscious level, constantly forming psychic frameworks beneath normal consciousness that can be used both by themselves and others in dream and trance states. They often appear to others in the dream condition, and they help dreamers in the manipulation of inner reality. They form images with which the dreamers can relate, images that can be used as bridges and then as gateways into kinds of consciousness more separated from your own.
quotes.translation: 他们能通过交流以更重要的含义影响他人。他们能轻松地从内在现实转向外在现实。他们本能地知道如何使用象征。他们在无意识层面高度创造，不断在正常意识之下形成心灵框架，这些框架可被他们自己和他人用于梦境和出神状态。他们常在梦境中出现在他人面前，并帮助做梦者操控内在现实。他们形成做梦者能关联的影像，这些影像可作为桥梁，进而成为通往与你自身更分离的意识类型的门户。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This is extremely important, since the dream world operates within the dimensions of your own psychic field, but utterly divorced from both space-time continuum and physical construction. Here you see the self truly spills over, not only into what you would call not-self, but into areas with which the conscious self is barely familiar. On an unconscious level however the self is very aware of the progress of these secondary personalities, and indeed uses this plane itself for the fulfillment and development of qualities originally attached to it, but incompatible with its main intents. The two planes constantly enrich and affect each other.
quotes.translation: 这极其重要，因为梦境世界在你自身心灵场的维度内运作，却完全脱离时空连续体和物理构造。在此，你看到自我真正溢出，不仅进入你所谓的非自我，还进入意识自我几乎不熟悉的领域。然而在无意识层面，自我非常清楚这些次要人格的进展，并确实利用这一层面本身来实现和发展最初附着于自身但与主要意图不相容的特质。这两个层面不断相互丰富和影响。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: In your present system of beliefs, and with the dubious light in which the unconscious is considered, a fear of the emotions is often generated. Not only are they often hindered in waking life, then, but censored as much as possible in dreams. Their expression becomes very difficult; great blockages of energy occur, which in your terms can result in neurotic or even stronger, psychotic, behavior.
quotes.translation: 在你们目前的信念体系中，由于对无意识的看法暧昧不明，往往会产生对情感的恐惧。因此，情感不仅在清醒生活中常受阻碍，在梦境中也尽可能被审查。它们的表达变得非常困难；巨大的能量阻塞发生，用你们的话说，可能导致神经症甚至更严重的精神病行为。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Applied to old age, the color black denotes a returning to those unconscious forces. Now all of this so far is from the standpoint of American and Western belief. It is simply the reality in which many of my readers are involved. In other "underground" systems of belief, however, black is seen as a symbol of great knowledge, power and strength. When this is carried to an extreme you wind up with devil cults, in which the poorly understood powers of creativity and exuberance rush out in distorted form; the undersides of consciousness are then glorified at the expense of the other, white, "conscious and objective" values.
quotes.translation: 应用于老年时，黑色象征着回归那些无意识的力量。到目前为止，所有这些都来自美国和西方信仰的立场。这仅仅是我的许多读者所涉入的现实。然而，在其他"地下"信仰体系中，黑色被视为伟大知识、力量和能力的象征。当这被推向极端时，你们就会陷入恶魔崇拜，其中那些被误解的创造力和旺盛力量以扭曲形式爆发出来；意识的阴暗面于是以牺牲另一面——白色、"有意识且客观"的价值——为代价而被颂扬。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: If you do this faithfully, within a month you will find the new conditions materializing in your experience. Your neurological structure will respond automatically. The unconscious will be aroused, bringing its great powers to bear, bringing you the new results. Do not try to overdo this, to go through the entire day worrying about beliefs, for example. This can only cause you to contrast what you have with what you want. Forget the exercise when it is completed. You will find yourself with impulses that arrive in line with these newly inserted beliefs, and then it is up to you to act on these and not ignore them.
quotes.translation: 如果你忠实地这样做，一个月内你就会发现新条件在你的经验中具体化。你的神经结构会自动响应。无意识将被唤醒，发挥其巨大力量，为你带来新结果。不要试图过度为之，例如整天担忧信念。这只会让你将现状与渴望进行对比。练习完成后就忘记它。你会发现与这些新植入信念一致的冲动出现，然后由你决定是否采取行动而非忽视它们。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (11:44.) The features in a portrait are painted on canvas or board, but your soul is not painted on your body. It enters into and becomes part of it. Physically, you cannot contain all of your identity, and that "free" portion unconsciously creates the flesh, in your terms. Again, you direct its form through your beliefs, but the unconscious part of you does the "work" of producing it.
quotes.translation: (11:44) 肖像的特征画在画布或画板上，但你的灵魂并非画在身体上。它进入并成为身体的一部分。从肉体上讲，你无法容纳你的全部身份，而那个"自由"部分在你们看来无意识地创造了肉体。再次强调，你通过信念指导其形态，但你的无意识部分完成了产生它的"工作"。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Natural hypnosis and conscious beliefs give their proper instructions to the unconscious, which then dutifully affects the body mechanism so that it responds in a manner harmonious with the beliefs. So you condition your body to react in certain fashions. Dealing with this is not a simple problem, of course, for the original suggestion of dis-ease was in itself given because of another belief. Using formal hypnosis, and in the West, you may regress and discover where the suggestion was first given you. If you and your hypnotist believe in reincarnation, the source may be discovered in another life.
quotes.translation: 自然催眠和意识信念向无意识发出适当指令，无意识随后忠实地影响身体机制，使其以与信念和谐的方式响应。因此，你条件化你的身体以特定方式反应。处理这个问题当然不是简单的事，因为疾病的最初暗示本身就是因为另一个信念而给出的。在西方，使用正式催眠，你可以回溯并发现最初给你暗示的地方。如果你和你的催眠师相信轮回，这个源头可能在另一世中被发现。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There are also even deeper biological connections with the heart of nature. You are biological creatures. Your proud human consciousness rests on the vast "unconscious" integrity of your physical being. In that regard your consciousness is as natural as your toe. In terms of the species' integrity your mental states are, then, highly important. Despair or apathy is a biological "enemy." Social conditions, political states, economic policies, and even religious or philosophical frameworks that foster such mental states, bring about a biological retaliation. They act like fire applied to a plant.
quotes.translation: 还有与自然之心更深的生物学联系。你们是生物性生物。你们骄傲的人类意识依赖于你们肉体存在的广阔"无意识"完整性。在这方面，你们的意识就像你们的脚趾一样自然。就物种完整性而言，你们的精神状态因此极为重要。绝望或冷漠是生物性的"敌人"。助长这类精神状态的社会条件、政治状态、经济政策，甚至宗教或哲学框架，都会招致生物性的报复。它们就像对植物施加的火焰。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Again: Your reality is like a shining platform, a surface resting upon probabilities. You follow these so unconsciously and beautifully, you swim through them so easily, that it does not occur to you to question your origin, or the medium in which your experience has its existence. All of those sharing any given birthdate, however, sharing even place as well as time, do not have the same "destiny"; but more, they do not share the same conditions necessarily. They are each affected by their own probability system at birth, and those conditions drastically alter the nature of their development.
quotes.translation: 再次强调：你们的实相如同一座闪亮的平台，一个建立在或然性之上的表面。你们如此无意识地、优美地遵循着这些或然性，如此轻松地穿梭其中，以至于从未想过质疑自己的起源，或质疑你们经验赖以存在的媒介。然而，所有共享同一出生日期的人，即便连时间和地点都相同，也并非拥有相同的"命运"；不仅如此，他们未必共享相同的条件。每个人在出生时都受到自身或然性系统的影响，而这些条件会彻底改变他们发展的本质。
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In your comparatively simple experience, you can see, however, the implications of such activities. Men may react to future events by unconsciously translating them into art, or motion pictures. They may react by unconsciously taking certain steps of a political nature that seem at the time either unreasonable, or even incomprehensible -- steps whose logic appears only in hindsight.
quotes.translation: 在你们相对简单的经验中，你们可以看到这类活动的含义。人们可能通过无意识地将未来事件转化为艺术或电影来对其做出反应。他们可能通过无意识地采取某些政治性质的步骤来做出反应，这些步骤在当时看来要么不合理，甚至不可理解——其逻辑只有在事后才显现出来。
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Love perceives the grace in another. Like natural guilt, the state of grace is unconscious in the animals. It is protected. They take it for granted, not knowing what it is or what they do, yet it speaks through all their motions and they dwell in the ancient wisdom of its ways. They do not have conscious memory, again, but the instinctive memory of the cells and organs sustains them. All of this applies in degrees according to the species, and when I speak of conscious memory I am using words that are familiar to you -- I mean a memory that can at any time look back through itself.
quotes.translation: 爱能感知他人身上的优雅。就像自然罪疚一样，优雅状态在动物身上是无意识的。它受到保护。动物们视其为理所当然，不知道它是什么，也不知道自己在做什么，然而它通过它们所有的动作表达自己，它们栖居在其方式的古老智慧中。它们没有意识记忆，但细胞和器官的本能记忆支撑着它们。这一切根据物种的不同而程度各异，当我谈到意识记忆时，我使用的是你们熟悉的词汇——我指的是那种可以随时回顾自身的记忆。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your sense data, again, will most definitely reinforce your ideas. You will also react clairvoyantly and telepathically to inner information at an unconscious level that is, once more, "collected" under the organization of your quite conscious concepts concerning existence in general, and your own in particular. So you are locked into physical situations that are corroborated by the great evidence of sense data -- and of course it is convincing because it reflects so beautifully, so creatively, and so actively, your own ideas and beliefs, whether they are positive or negative.
quotes.translation: 再说一次，你们的感官资料绝对会强化你们的观念。你们还会在无意识层面上以千里眼和心灵感应的方式对内在信息做出反应，这些信息同样是在你们关于一般存在和自身存在的相当有意识的观念组织下"收集"起来的。因此，你们被锁定在由感官资料的巨大证据所证实的物质情境中——当然这很有说服力，因为它如此优美、如此富有创造力、如此活跃地反映了你们自己的观念和信念，无论它们是正面的还是负面的。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ruburt does extremely well with interior sound, and so I use that method rather than, say, an image to make my independent existence known. Now Ruburt called me originally (last Saturday night) at unconscious levels because he was upset with "earth programming." He thought that you needed some help from the outside, so to speak. That intent set up certain signals that reached into other realities or stations, and I answered. I was not speaking to Ruburt personally when he heard me, but addressing myself to the world at large in a program that was indeed picked up by others.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏在处理内在声音方面做得非常好，所以我使用那种方法，而不是比如说图像，来表明我的独立存在。现在，鲁柏最初（上周六晚上）在无意识层面上召唤我，是因为他对"地球编程"感到不安。他认为你们需要一些来自外界的帮助，可以这么说。那个意图建立了某些信号，触及了其他实相或站点，而我回应了。当他听到我时，我并不是在对鲁柏个人说话，而是在一个确实被其他人接收到的节目中，向整个世界发表讲话。
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I do not want to minimize the importance of your state of consciousness; as, for example, you read this book. Presumably you are awake, but in many ways when you are awake, you are resting far more than you are in your so-called unconscious nightly state. Then to a larger extent you realize your own reality, and are free to use abilities that in the daytime you ignore or deny.
quotes.translation: 我不想低估你们意识状态的重要性；例如，当你们阅读这本书时。想必你们是清醒的，但在许多方面，当你们清醒时，你们休息的程度远超过你们所谓的夜间无意识状态。那时你们在更大程度上认识到自己的实相，并且可以自由使用那些在白天被你们忽视或否认的能力。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (8:58.) During this period, incidentally, mental activity of the highest, most original variety was the strongest dream characteristic, and the knowledge [man] gained was imprinted upon the physical brain: what is now completely unconscious activity involving the functions of the body, its relationship with the environment, its balance and temperature, its constant inner alterations. All of these highly intricate activities were learned and practiced in the dream state as the CU's translated their inner knowledge through the state of dreaming into the physical form.
quotes.translation: （8点58分。）在此期间，最高级、最原创的心理活动是梦境最显著的特征，人类获得的知识被烙印在物质大脑上：如今完全无意识的活动涉及身体功能、与环境的关系、平衡与温度、持续的内在变化。所有这些高度复杂的活动都是在梦境状态中学习和实践的，因为CU通过梦境状态将内在知识转化为物质形态。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The "true facts" are that you exist in this life and outside it simultaneously. You are "between lives" and "in lives" at once. The deeper dimensions of reality are such that your thoughts and actions not only affect the life you know, but also reach into all of those other simultaneous existences. What you think now is unconsciously perceived by some hypothetical 14th-century self. The psyche is open-ended. No system is closed, psychological systems least of all. Your life is a dreaming experience to other portions of your greater reality which focus elsewhere.
quotes.translation: "真实情况"是，你同时存在于这一生及其之外。你同时"处于生命之间"和"处于生命之中"。现实的更深维度使得你的思想和行动不仅影响你所知的生命，还触及所有其他同时存在。你现在的想法被某个假设的14世纪自我无意识地感知。心灵是开放的。没有系统是封闭的，心理系统尤其如此。你的生命对你更大实相中聚焦于别处的其他部分而言，是一场梦境体验。
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The most private agonies of the soul were assigned a more or less common source in man's primitive "unconscious" drives. The private unquelled thrusts toward creativity were seen as the unbalanced conglomeration of chemicals within a person's most private being -- a twist of perversity. Genius was seen as a mistake of chromosomes, or the fortunate result of a man's hatred for his father. The meaning of life was reduced to the accidental nature of genes. Science thought in terms of averages and statistics, and each person was supposed to fit within those realms.
quotes.translation: 灵魂最私密的痛苦被归因于人类原始"无意识"驱力的某种共同来源。那些未被压制的、朝向创造力的私人冲动，被视为一个人最私密存在中化学物质的不平衡聚合——一种反常的扭曲。天才被视为染色体的错误，或一个人对父亲仇恨的幸运结果。生命的意义被简化为基因的偶然性。科学以平均值和统计数字思考，每个人都应被纳入这些范畴。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What sin did the poor person or the sick person commit? That question, often asked unconsciously -- if not consciously -- brings you back to beliefs in punishment that have nothing to do with the concept of natural guilt, but with those distortions placed upon it. There is also a connection with misinterpretation of the Bible. Christ as you think of him was simply saying that you form your own reality. He tried to rise above the idea-systems of those times, yet even he had to use them, and so the connotations of sin and punishment distorted the message given.
quotes.translation: 穷人或有病的人犯了什么罪？这个问题——即使不是有意识地，也常常被无意识地提出——让你回到与惩罚相关的信念，这些信念与自然罪的概念无关，而是与强加于其上的扭曲有关。这也与对圣经的误解有关。你所认为的基督只是在说，你创造了自己的实相。他试图超越那个时代的观念体系，但即使他也不得不使用它们，因此罪与惩罚的涵义扭曲了他所传达的信息。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Jung was correct in postulating a collective unconscious. But with his limited knowledge he did not see that this unconscious would exist outside of your three-dimensional system entirely, holding future as well as past, nor that it has such a cohesive effect upon humanity as a whole. It is the one self with its origins within your system, but its existence outside.
quotes.translation: 荣格假设集体无意识是正确的。但由于知识有限，他没有看到这种无意识完全存在于你的三维系统之外，既包含未来也包含过去，也没有看到它对整个人类具有如此凝聚性的影响。它是那个自我，其起源在你的系统之内，但其存在却在系统之外。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Still, to the unconscious the problem has been set and given. In the following deep protected areas of sleep, the higher centers of the inner self are allowed to function and come to the aid of the three-dimensionally oriented portion of the personality. This more liberated self sees the situation much more clearly, suggests a given line of action (but does not order it), and informs the dreaming self. The dreaming self then manufactures a group of dreams in which the solution is stated within a symbolic dream situation.
quotes.translation: 然而，问题已被设定并交给无意识。在接下来的深层受保护睡眠区域，内在自我的更高中心被允许运作，并帮助人格中面向三维的部分。这个更自由的自我更清晰地看到情况，建议一条特定的行动路线（但不命令它），并告知做梦的自我。做梦的自我随后制造一组梦境，在其中解决方案被陈述于一个象征性的梦境情境中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Many of the most powerful aspects of consciousness are at work precisely when it seems to you that you are relatively unconscious and asleep to physical reality. It would be impossible for you to handle the vast amount of material available, in the context of time as you presently experience it. To operate adequately in your highly specific field (pause), an almost infinite amount of information must be instantly assimilated, probabilities calculated, and certain balances maintained of which you are not even aware.
quotes.translation: 意识最强大的许多层面，恰恰在你看似相对无意识、对物质实相沉睡时运作。若以你目前体验的时间框架而言，你根本不可能处理如此庞大的可用信息。要在你高度特定的领域（停顿）中适当运作，几乎无限的信息必须被瞬间吸收，概率必须被计算，某些平衡必须被维持——而你甚至对此毫无察觉。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now: An open fire elicits certain responses from the cells4 that, for instance, a furnace does not. The effect of the light plus the warmth on the skin is extremely healing. People sit by a fireplace in wintertime because it is unconsciously recognized that recuperative and therapeutic results occur. Simply put, the cells respond to firelight in somewhat the same manner that flowers do to sunlight. The stimulation is much more than skin deep, however, and an open fire is cleansing. It even helps clear the blood.
quotes.translation: 现在：明火会引发细胞特定的反应，例如暖气炉就不会。光线加上温暖对皮肤产生的效果极具疗愈力。冬天人们坐在壁炉旁，是因为潜意识中认识到这能带来恢复与治疗效果。简单来说，细胞对火光反应的方式，某种程度上就像花朵对阳光的反应。然而这种刺激远非表面，明火具有净化作用，甚至有助于清洁血液。
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the natural body-mind relationship the sleep state operates as a great connector, an interpreter, allowing the free flow of conscious and unconscious material. In the kind of sleep patterns suggested, optimum conditions are set up. Neurosis and psychosis simply would not occur under such conditions. And in the natural back-and-forth leeway of the system, exterior dilemmas or problems are worked out in the dream situation, and interior difficulty may also be solved symbolically through physical experience.
quotes.translation: 在自然的身心关系中，睡眠状态扮演着伟大的连接者与诠释者角色，允许意识与无意识材料自由流动。在建议的睡眠模式中，最佳条件得以建立。神经症与精神病在此条件下根本不会发生。而在系统自然的来回弹性中，外在困境或问题会在梦境情境中得到解决，内在困难也可能通过物质体验以象征方式化解。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Because his conscious desire was based strongly upon inner emotional need, and not opposed to it, and because the emotional need at that time was powerful, that is his need to leave on vacation, and because he remembered our discussion on expectation, he was able to utilize both conscious and unconscious energies. In other words, to consciously focus his subconscious psychic abilities to perform toward a definite, material end.
quotes.translation: 因为他的意识渴望强烈基于内在情感需求且未与之对立，又因当时情感需求强烈——即他需要去度假——且他记得我们关于期待的讨论，他得以同时运用意识与无意识能量。换言之，有意识地将潜意识心灵能力聚焦，以朝向明确物质目标运作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Experience is the product of the mind, the spirit, conscious thoughts and feelings, and unconscious thoughts and feelings. These together form the reality that you know. You are hardly at the mercy of a reality, therefore, that exists apart from yourself, or is thrust upon you. You are so intimately connected with the physical events composing your life experience that often you cannot distinguish between the seemingly material occurrences and the thoughts, expectations and desires that gave them birth.
quotes.translation: 体验是心灵、精神、意识思想与感受、无意识思想与感受的共同产物。这些共同构成你所知的实相。因此你绝非受制于一个独立于你之外或强加于你的实相。你与构成人生经历的物质事件如此紧密相连，以至于常无法区分看似物质性的事件与孕育它们的想法、期待和渴望。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Referring to his Introduction again, he remarks that he misses the excitement of the chase, encountered in his own creative work. Here, you see, the acceleration is so rapid and intense that he is not consciously able to follow it. The unconscious, so-called, has little to do with this phenomenon. It is, however, related strongly to qualities inherent within each consciousness. Such ability is rarely used to full advantage. Connections are made so rapidly that the physical brain is not aware of them.
quotes.translation: 再次引用他的引言，他提到怀念自己创作工作中遭遇的追逐兴奋感。你看，这里加速如此迅速强烈，以至于他有意识无法跟上。所谓的无意识与此现象关联甚微。然而，这与每个意识内在固有的特质密切相关。这种能力极少被充分利用。连接建立得如此之快，以至于物质大脑无法察觉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You consciously give the signals for reaction. It is not the other way around. Past events do not intrude in this manner unless they are beckoned by the conscious expectations and thoughts that exist within your mind. (Pause.) Those unconscious memories will be activated according to your current beliefs. You will be replenished and renewed as your thoughts motivate joyful body sensations and physical events, or you will be depressed as you bring into your awareness unpleasant past body happenings.
quotes.translation: 你是有意识地发出反应信号的。并非反过来。过去的事件不会以这种方式侵入，除非它们被你心智中存在的有意识的期望和思想所召唤。（停顿）那些无意识的记忆会根据你当前的信念被激活。当你的思想激发愉悦的身体感受和物质事件时，你会得到补充和更新；或者当你将不愉快的过往身体经历带入觉知时，你会感到沮丧。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In man, conscious thoughts are highly important as the directors of unconscious activity. You become more responsible, then, in a particular way for physical effects that, comparatively speaking, are "instinctive" in the animal. This gives you both a conscious and unconscious feedback system against which to test your experience and alter its nature.
quotes.translation: 在人类身上，有意识的思想作为无意识活动的指导者极其重要。因此，你以特定方式对物理效应承担更多责任——这些效应在动物身上相对而言是"本能的"。这为你提供了有意识和无意识的反馈系统，用以检验你的经验并改变其性质。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You are not a forsaken offshoot of physical matter, nor is your consciousness meant to vanish like a puff of smoke. Instead, you form the physical body that you know at a deeply unconscious level with great discrimination, miraculous clarity, and intimate unconscious knowledge of each minute cell that composes it. This is not meant symbolically.
quotes.translation: 你并非被遗弃的物质衍生物，你的意识也并非注定如烟消散。相反，你在深层的无意识层面以非凡的辨识力、奇迹般的清晰度，以及对构成身体的每个微小细胞亲密无间的无意识知识，形成了你所知的物质身体。这并非象征性的说法。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inhibition of such emotions also interferes with the nervous system and its therapeutic devices. These repressed emotions, and the whole charge behind such distorted concepts about the unconscious, result in a projection outward upon others. In your individual area there will be persons upon whom you will project all of those charged, frightening emotions or characteristics. At the same time you will be drawn to those individuals because the projections represent a part of you.
quotes.translation: 压抑此类情绪也会干扰神经系统及其治疗机制。这些被压抑的情绪，以及关于无意识的扭曲概念背后的全部能量，会导致向外投射到他人身上。在你的个人领域里，会有某些人成为你投射所有那些充满能量的、可怕的情绪或特征的对象。同时你也会被这些人吸引，因为这些投射代表了你的一部分。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I call this seemingly unconscious the "inner ego," for it directs inner activities. It correlates information that is perceived not through the physical senses, but through other inner channels. It is the inner perceiver of reality that exists beyond the three-dimensional. It carries within it the memory of each of your past existences. It looks into subjective dimensions that are literally infinite, and from these subjective dimensions all objective realities flow. (Long pause.)
quotes.translation: 我将这个看似无意识的部分称为"内在自我"，因为它指导着内在活动。它整合那些并非通过物质感官，而是通过其他内在渠道感知的信息。它是存在于三维之外实相的内在感知者。它携带着你每一世存在的记忆。它审视着真正无限的主观维度，而所有客观实相都从这些主观维度中流出。（长停顿）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The conditions, however, while "set" in one fashion, are highly plastic in another, so that a multitudinous variety of probable events can flow from them. Precognitively you are unconsciously quite aware -- again in your terms -- of the results of any given action or cause. When this (photograph) of Ruburt3 was taken, he had already become aware of the overall interests and concerns that would dominate his future life, although the particular course of it had not been chosen.
quotes.translation: 然而，这些条件虽然以某种方式"设定"，在另一方面却具有高度可塑性，因此无数种可能事件可以从它们中涌现。预知性地看，你在无意识层面相当清楚——再次用你的说法——任何特定行动或原因的结果。当这张（鲁柏的）照片被拍摄时，他已经意识到将主导他未来生活的整体兴趣和关注点，尽管具体的人生道路尚未选定。
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Instinctively you leave your body for varying amounts of time each night while you sleep, but those journeys are not "programmed." You plan your own tours, in other words. As many people with the same interests may decide to visit the same country together, on tour, so in the out-of-body condition you may travel alone or with companions. If you are alert you may even take snapshots -- only as far as inner tours are concerned, the snapshots consist of clear pictures of the environment taken at the time, developed in the unconscious, and then presented to the waking mind.
quotes.translation: 你本能地每晚在睡眠中离开身体一段时间，但这些旅程并非"预设程序"。换句话说，你自行规划旅程。就像许多志趣相投的人可能决定结伴游览同一个国家，在出体状态下，你可以独自旅行或与同伴同行。如果你保持警觉，甚至能拍下快照——就内在旅程而言，这些快照由当时环境的清晰画面构成，在无意识中显影，随后呈现给清醒意识。
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He knew that he was ready to go on to other spheres of activity. Unconsciously, he looked about for the means and chose those immediately available. This particular individual, three days earlier, had made the plan. There was no predestination involved. Because a tree branch falls, this does not mean that it was destined to fall in either the particular manner of its fall nor in the timing of the fall. There is a great difference between free choice and predestination.
quotes.translation: 他知道自己已准备好进入其他活动领域。无意识地，他环顾四周寻找途径，选择了那些立即可用的。这个特定个体在三天前就已制定计划。其中并无宿命成分。树枝坠落，并不意味着它的坠落方式或时机是命中注定的。自由选择与宿命论之间存在巨大差异。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The conscious mind is a maker of distinctions. It brings to the surface of awareness whole gestalts of previously unconscious material, then assembles and organizes it in ever-changing form. Through purposeful focus, a literally infinite amount of such data can be unconsciously sorted; then only the desired elements will emerge.
quotes.translation: 意识心是区分者。它将先前无意识材料的完整完形带到意识表层，然后以不断变化的形式进行组装和整理。通过有目的的聚焦，无限量的此类数据可以被无意识分类；随后只有所需的元素才会浮现。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Often in the aged you find such frameworks coming into being naturally, but those who awaken spontaneously after four hours consider themselves insomniacs because of their beliefs, and so cannot utilize their experience properly. Both the conscious and unconscious would operate far more effectively, however, under an abbreviated sleeping program, and for those involved in "creative" endeavors this kind of schedule would bring greater intuition and applied knowledge.
quotes.translation: 在老年人中，你常发现这种框架自然形成，但那些四小时后自然醒来的人，因信念而自认失眠，无法妥善运用其经验。然而，在缩短的睡眠模式下，意识与无意识都会更高效运作，对从事"创造性"工作的人而言，这种作息会带来更强的直觉与应用知识。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: First of all, to me the term "unconscious" is a poor one, barely hinting at an actual open psychic system, with deep intertwining roots uniting all kinds of consciousness; a network in which we are all connected. Our individuality rises out of it, but also helps form it. This source contains past, present, and future information; only the ego experiencing time as we know it. I also believe that this open system contains other kinds of consciousness beside our own.
quotes.translation: 首先，对我而言"无意识"这个术语很糟糕，它几乎未能暗示一个实际开放的心灵系统，其深层交织的根系联结着各种意识；一个我们彼此相连的网络。我们的个体性从中升起，同时也帮助形成它。这个源头包含过去、现在和未来的信息；只有自我在体验我们所知的时间。我还相信，这个开放系统包含除我们自身之外的其他意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your own idea of the perilous nature of existence becomes so strong that the ego allows this data to emerge, even though it is "out of time," because your fearful beliefs convince it that you must be on guard. The incidents do not even have to involve you. From all the unconscious telepathic and clairvoyant data available, however, you will be aware of this particular grouping, and it will only serve to reinforce your idea that existence is above all perilous.
quotes.translation: 你对存在之危险本质的看法变得如此强烈，以至于自我允许这些数据浮现，即使它们"超越时间"，因为你恐惧的信念说服你必须保持警惕。这些事件甚至不必涉及你。然而，从所有可得的无意识心电感应与千里眼数据中，你会注意到这个特定集合，它只会强化你的观念：存在本质上是危险的。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Many writers of historical pieces, for example, are writing out of direct experience with those times. Such instances represent an excellent working rapport between the present self and the unconscious, which brings these memories to the surface in such a way that current life is enriched. More often than not, true awareness of the situation often becomes almost conscious, and just beneath awareness the individual knows the source of his authentic material.
quotes.translation: 例如，许多历史题材的作家，其写作素材直接源于与那些时代的亲身经历。这类案例展现了当下自我与无意识之间的卓越协作关系——无意识将这些记忆带到表层，从而丰富当前的生活。通常情况下，对情境的真实觉知几乎会进入意识层面，而个体在意识表层之下，始终知晓其真实素材的来源。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: At the same time all beliefs are communicated to others, not only through quite unconscious bodily mechanisms, but telepathically. You will always try to correlate your ideas with exterior experience. (Pause.) All of the abilities of the inner self will be brought to bear to materialize the image of your beliefs, regardless of what they ought to be. The "proper" emotions will be generated, bringing about those body states that exist in your conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 与此同时，所有信念不仅通过完全无意识的身体机制，还通过心电感应传达给他人。你总会试图将自己的想法与外在经验相协调。（停顿）内在自我的所有能力都将被调动起来，以具现你信念的意象——无论这些信念"应该"是什么。相应的"恰当"情绪将被激发，从而产生与你意识心智中存在的身体状态。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: On the other hand, areas of ordinary behavior that may have seemed opaque before, cloudy or dark -- personal characteristic behavior that was not understood, for instance -- may suddenly become quite clear as a result of this transformation, in which the shadowy aspects of the unconscious are perceived as brilliant.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，那些原本可能显得晦涩、模糊或黑暗的日常行为领域——例如不被理解的个人特质行为——可能会因这种转化而突然变得清晰，此时无意识的阴影面被视作璀璨的光明。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (11:40) To some extent there is also an assault upon simple creaturehood. Its images and experience, furthermore, are seldom forgotten, and the so-called new ego is born with the memory of their imprint. Some psychologists like to say that you cry out unconsciously against the natural method of your birth.* But here you have the situation where a self is faced with its own annihilation, while another "self" arises after conscious participation with its death.
quotes.translation: （11:40）在某种程度上，这也构成了对纯粹生物本性的冲击。此外，其意象与经验鲜少被遗忘，所谓的新自我诞生时便带着这些印记的记忆。有些心理学家喜欢说，你无意识地抗拒着出生的自然方式*。但这里的情况是：一个自我面临自身的湮灭，而另一个"自我"在意识参与其死亡后诞生。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: After death, for example, an individual may continue to create these -- masses of individuals may -- until they realize that the frameworks are no longer necessary. The Speakers were not confined in their activities, therefore, to waking consciousness. In all periods of your time they went about their duties both in the waking and sleep state. Much of the most pertinent information, in fact, was memorized by trainees during the dream condition, and passed on in the same manner. These unwritten manuscripts therefore were also illustrated, so to speak, by dream journeys or field trips into other kinds of reality. Such training still goes on. The particular psychic or story framework may vary. For example, conventional images of the Christian God and the saints may be utilized by the Speakers, with all of this highly vivid. The dreamer may find himself then in a magnificent harem, or instead in a brilliantly illuminated field or sky. Some Speakers confine their abilities to the dream state; and, waking, are largely unconscious of their own abilities or experience.
quotes.translation: 例如，死后个体可能继续创造这些——群体可能如此——直到他们意识到这些框架不再必要。因此，传讯者的活动并不局限于清醒意识。在你们时间的各个时期，他们都在清醒与睡眠状态中履行职责。事实上，许多最相关的信息是在梦境状态中被受训者记忆，并以同样方式传承。这些未成文的手稿，可以说，也通过梦境之旅或进入其他实相领域的实地考察得到阐释。此类训练仍在继续。特定的心理或故事框架可能有所不同。例如，基督教上帝与圣徒的传统意象可能被传讯者运用，且极为生动。做梦者可能发现自己身处宏伟的后宫，或是在光芒万丈的原野或天空。有些传讯者将能力局限于梦境状态；清醒时，他们对自己的能力或经验几乎毫无意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Beyond this there are experiences but seldom recalled, in which the usual identification of your consciousness with physical-life orientation is gone. (Pause.) Images as you think of them are based upon your own neurological structure, and your interpretations of these. When you consider survival after death, for instance, you imagine all the senses fully operating, though perhaps in a nonphysical body. Perception without images seems impossible in that context. Yet in some dream situations you enter a state of awareness quite divorced from that kind of sense data. Images as such are not involved, though later they may be manufactured unconsciously for the sake of translation. In those conditions you come close to an understanding of what your consciousness is when it is not physically oriented at all.
quotes.translation: 除此之外，还有鲜少被忆起的经验，其中你意识与物质生命取向的惯常认同消失了。（停顿）你所理解的意象基于自身的神经结构及其诠释。例如，当你考虑死后存续时，会想象所有感官完全运作——尽管或许在非物质的躯体中。在此语境下，没有意象的感知似乎不可能。然而在某些梦境情境中，你进入一种完全脱离此类感官数据的觉知状态。意象本身并不参与其中，尽管后来可能为翻译之故而无意识地制造出来。在这些状态下，你接近理解当意识完全不具物质取向时，它究竟是什么。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The physical reality into which you are born is not nearly as solid or predetermined or definite as it appears to be. Instead there is a field of rich interaction. Your consciousness must be focused at one particular range of frequencies before it can even perceive matter, much less solidity. In sleep your consciousness fluctuates between ranges of intensities, literally flowing into and out of the physical-matter grouping, and forming from more plastic "pre-matter" (with a hyphen) stages, the final shape that matter will take in your world. The same applies to events, where some will be crystallized in physical terms and others will not. The deep portions of your own being are aware of those purposes and intents that are uniquely yours. Unconsciously, then, you have within you what you might think of as a set of blueprints for the particular kind of physical reality you want to materialize. You are the architect.
quotes.translation: 你出生的物质实相并不像表面看起来那样坚固、预定或确定。相反，它是一片丰富的互动场域。你的意识必须聚焦在某个特定的频率范围，才能感知物质，更不用说坚固性了。在睡眠中，你的意识在强度范围之间波动，实际上是在流入和流出物质群组，并从更具可塑性的“前物质”（带连字符）阶段，形成物质在你世界中最终呈现的形状。事件也是如此，有些会以物质形式结晶，有些则不会。你存在的深处知晓那些属于你独特的目的和意图。因此，在你无意识中，你拥有你所想物质化的特定物质实相的一套蓝图。你是建筑师。
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In other terms, however -- social terms -- you have yet to achieve the same kind of spiritual brotherhood possessed by your cells; and so you do not understand that the experience of your world is intimately connected with your own private experience. If you burn your finger it hurts immediately. Your body instantly begins a cooperative venture, in which adjustments are made so that the wound begins to heal. If a portion of the race is hurt it may take a while before "you" feel the pain, but the entire unconscious mechanism of the species will try to heal the wound. Consciously you can facilitate that development, and admit your brotherhood with all other living beings. The healing will take place far quicker if you do. A biological brotherhood exists, an inner empathy on cellular levels, connecting all individuals of the species with one another. This is the result of a biological idealization. It exists in all species, and connects all species.
quotes.translation: 然而，从其他角度——社会角度——你尚未达到你的细胞所拥有的那种灵性兄弟情谊；因此你不明白，你世界的经验与你个人的私密经验紧密相连。如果你烧伤手指，它会立即疼痛。你的身体立刻开始一项合作事业，进行调整以使伤口开始愈合。如果种族的一部分受伤，可能需要一段时间“你”才会感到疼痛，但整个物种的无意识机制会试图愈合伤口。你有意识地可以促进这一发展，并承认你与所有其他生命体的兄弟情谊。如果你这样做，愈合会快得多。存在一种生物兄弟情谊，一种细胞层面的内在共情，连接着物种的所有个体。这是生物理想化的结果。它存在于所有物种中，并连接所有物种。
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What is required is more than a shedding of clothes, however. To plunge into this ocean you also leave the physical body at the shore. It will be there when you get back. Your camouflage patterns can be likened to those cast by sun and shadow upon the ever-moving waves. As long as you keep the pattern in mind, you create it, and it is there. If you turn your head away for a moment and then look quickly back you can see only the wave. Your camouflage and your world are created by conscious focusing and unconscious concentration.
